authors_text: Shijing Yuan,Hongze Liu,Hongtao Lv,Zhanbo Feng,Jie Li,Hongyang Chen,Chentao Wu
title: Adaptive incentive for cross-silo federated learning: A multi-agent reinforcement learning approach
title_zh: 跨孤岛联邦学习的自适应激励：一种多智能体强化学习方法
abstract: Cross-silo federated learning (FL) is a typical FL that enables organizations(e.g., financial or medical entities) to train global models on isolated data. Reasonable incentive is key to encouraging organizations to contribute data. However, existing works on incentivizing cross-silo FL lack consideration of the environmental dynamics (e.g., precision of the trained global model and data owned by uncertain clients during the training processes). Moreover, most of them assume that organizations share private information, which is unrealistic. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel adaptive mechanism for cross-silo FL, towards incentivizing organizations to contribute data to maximize their long-term payoffs in a real dynamic training environment. The mechanism is based on multi-agent reinforcement learning, which learns near-optimal data contribution strategy from the history of potential games without organizations' private information. Experiments demonstrate that our mechanism achieves adaptive incentive and effectively improves the long-term payoffs for organizations.
summary: Cross-silo federated learning (FL) is a typical FL that enables organizations(e.g., financial or medical entities) to train global models on isolated data. Reasonable incentive is key to encouraging organizations to contribute data. However, existing works on incentivizing cross-silo FL lack consideration of the environmental dynamics (e.g., precision of the trained global model and data owned by uncertain clients during the training processes). Moreover, most of them assume that organizations share private information, which is unrealistic. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel adaptive mechanism for cross-silo FL, towards incentivizing organizations to contribute data to maximize their long-term payoffs in a real dynamic training environment. The mechanism is based on multi-agent reinforcement learning, which learns near-optimal data contribution strategy from the history of potential games without organizations' private information. Experiments demonstrate that our mechanism achieves adaptive incentive and effectively improves the long-term payoffs for organizations.
abstract_zh: 跨孤岛联邦学习 (FL) 是一种典型的 FL，它使组织（例如金融或医疗实体）能够基于孤立数据训练全局模型。合理的激励是鼓励组织贡献数据的关键。然而，现有的激励跨筒仓 FL 的工作缺乏对环境动态的考虑（例如，训练后的全局模型的精度以及训练过程中不确定客户拥有的数据）。此外，他们中的大多数人假设组织共享私人信息，这是不现实的。为了克服这些限制，我们提出了一种新颖的跨筒仓 FL 自适应机制，旨在激励组织贡献数据，以在真实的动态培训环境中最大化其长期回报。该机制基于多智能体强化学习，在没有组织私人信息的情况下从潜在博弈的历史中学习接近最优的数据贡献策略。实验表明，我们的机制实现了自适应激励，有效提高了组织的长期收益。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于多智能体强化学习的自适应激励机制，用于解决跨机构联邦学习中的数据贡献问题。
AI.motivation: 现有跨机构联邦学习的激励机制未考虑训练环境动态变化（如模型精度和客户端数据不确定性），且假设组织共享私有信息，不符合实际场景。
AI.method: 设计多智能体强化学习框架，通过潜在博弈历史学习近似最优数据贡献策略，无需获取组织私有信息。
AI.result: 实验表明该机制能适应动态环境，有效提升组织长期收益，优于传统激励方法。
AI.conclusion: 提出的自适应激励机制克服了现有方法的局限性，在无需私有信息的前提下实现了跨机构联邦学习的高效协作。

authors_text: Catherine Yeh,Nedim Lipka,Franck Dernoncourt
title: Envisioning the Next-Gen Document Reader
title_zh: 设想下一代文档阅读器
comment: Paper accepted at the AAAI 2023 Workshop on Scientific Document Understanding
comments: Paper accepted at the AAAI 2023 Workshop on Scientific Document Understanding
abstract: People read digital documents on a daily basis to share, exchange, and understand information in electronic settings. However, current document readers create a static, isolated reading experience, which does not support users' goals of gaining more knowledge and performing additional tasks through document interaction. In this work, we present our vision for the next-gen document reader that strives to enhance user understanding and create a more connected, trustworthy information experience. We describe 18 NLP-powered features to add to existing document readers and propose a novel plug-in marketplace that allows users to further customize their reading experience, as demonstrated through 3 exploratory UI prototypes available at https://github.com/catherinesyeh/nextgen-prototypes
summary: People read digital documents on a daily basis to share, exchange, and understand information in electronic settings. However, current document readers create a static, isolated reading experience, which does not support users' goals of gaining more knowledge and performing additional tasks through document interaction. In this work, we present our vision for the next-gen document reader that strives to enhance user understanding and create a more connected, trustworthy information experience. We describe 18 NLP-powered features to add to existing document readers and propose a novel plug-in marketplace that allows users to further customize their reading experience, as demonstrated through 3 exploratory UI prototypes available at https://github.com/catherinesyeh/nextgen-prototypes
abstract_zh: 人们每天阅读数字文档，以在电子环境中共享、交换和理解信息。然而，当前的文档阅读器创建了静态的、孤立的阅读体验，这不支持用户通过文档交互获取更多知识和执行额外任务的目标。在这项工作中，我们提出了下一代文档阅读器的愿景，致力于增强用户理解并创造更加互联、值得信赖的信息体验。我们描述了 18 个 NLP 支持的功能，以添加到现有的文档阅读器中，并提出了一个新颖的插件市场，允许用户进一步定制他们的阅读体验，如 https://github.com/catherinesyeh/nextgen-prototypes 上提供的 3 个探索性 UI 原型所示

search_text: Envisioning the Next-Gen Document Reader 设想下一代文档阅读器 People read digital documents on a daily basis to share, exchange, and understand information in electronic settings. However, current document readers create a static, isolated reading experience, which does not support users' goals of gaining more knowledge and performing additional tasks through document interaction. authors_text: Meng Liu,Ke Liang,Yawei Zhao,Wenxuan Tu,Sihang Zhou,Xinbiao Gan,Xinwang Liu,Kunlun He
title: Self-Supervised Temporal Graph learning with Temporal and Structural Intensity Alignment
title_zh: 具有时间和结构强度对齐的自监督时间图学习
abstract: Temporal graph learning aims to generate high-quality representations for graph-based tasks with dynamic information, which has recently garnered increasing attention. In contrast to static graphs, temporal graphs are typically organized as node interaction sequences over continuous time rather than an adjacency matrix. Most temporal graph learning methods model current interactions by incorporating historical neighborhood. However, such methods only consider first-order temporal information while disregarding crucial high-order structural information, resulting in suboptimal performance. To address this issue, we propose a self-supervised method called S2T for temporal graph learning, which extracts both temporal and structural information to learn more informative node representations. Notably, the initial node representations combine first-order temporal and high-order structural information differently to calculate two conditional intensities. An alignment loss is then introduced to optimize the node representations, narrowing the gap between the two intensities and making them more informative. Concretely, in addition to modeling temporal information using historical neighbor sequences, we further consider structural knowledge at both local and global levels. At the local level, we generate structural intensity by aggregating features from high-order neighbor sequences. At the global level, a global representation is generated based on all nodes to adjust the structural intensity according to the active statuses on different nodes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed model S2T achieves at most 10.13% performance improvement compared with the state-of-the-art competitors on several datasets.
summary: Temporal graph learning aims to generate high-quality representations for graph-based tasks with dynamic information, which has recently garnered increasing attention. In contrast to static graphs, temporal graphs are typically organized as node interaction sequences over continuous time rather than an adjacency matrix. Most temporal graph learning methods model current interactions by incorporating historical neighborhood. However, such methods only consider first-order temporal information while disregarding crucial high-order structural information, resulting in suboptimal performance. To address this issue, we propose a self-supervised method called S2T for temporal graph learning, which extracts both temporal and structural information to learn more informative node representations. Notably, the initial node representations combine first-order temporal and high-order structural information differently to calculate two conditional intensities. An alignment loss is then introduced to optimize the node representations, narrowing the gap between the two intensities and making them more informative. Concretely, in addition to modeling temporal information using historical neighbor sequences, we further consider structural knowledge at both local and global levels. At the local level, we generate structural intensity by aggregating features from high-order neighbor sequences. At the global level, a global representation is generated based on all nodes to adjust the structural intensity according to the active statuses on different nodes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed model S2T achieves at most 10.13% performance improvement compared with the state-of-the-art competitors on several datasets.
abstract_zh: 时态图学习旨在为具有动态信息的基于图的任务生成高质量的表示，最近引起了越来越多的关注。与静态图相反，时间图通常被组织为连续时间内的节点交互序列而不是邻接矩阵。大多数时间图学习方法通​​过合并历史邻域来模拟当前的交互。然而，此类方法仅考虑一阶时间信息，而忽略关键的高阶结构信息，导致性能不佳。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种称为 S2T 的自监督方法，用于时间图学习，该方法提取时间和结构信息以学习更多信息节点表示。值得注意的是，初始节点表示以不同方式组合一阶时间和高阶结构信息来计算两个条件强度。然后引入对齐损失来优化节点表示，缩小两个强度之间的差距并使它们提供更多信息。具体来说，除了使用历史邻居序列对时间信息进行建模之外，我们还进一步考虑局部和全局层面的结构知识。在局部层面，我们通过聚合高阶邻居序列的特征来生成结构强度。在全局层面，基于所有节点生成全局表示，以根据不同节点上的活动状态调整结构强度。大量实验表明，与最先进的竞争对手相比，所提出的 S2T 模型在多个数据集上最多实现了 10.13% 的性能提升。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种自监督时序图学习方法S2T，通过结合时序和结构信息提升动态图的节点表示质量。
AI.motivation: 现有时序图学习方法仅关注一阶时序信息，忽略了高阶结构信息，导致表示效果不佳。
AI.method: 设计S2T框架，通过计算时序强度和结构强度的条件分布，并引入对齐损失函数缩小两者差距。同时结合局部高阶邻居聚合和全局节点状态调整结构强度。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上相比现有方法性能提升最高达10.13%。
AI.conclusion: 通过时序与结构强度对齐的自监督学习，S2T能更全面捕捉动态图的时序演化与拓扑结构特征。

authors_text: Shihan Liu,Junlin Zha,Jian Sun,Zhuo Li,Gang Wang
title: EdgeYOLO: An Edge-Real-Time Object Detector
title_zh: EdgeYOLO：边缘实时物体检测器
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient, low-complexity and anchor-free object detector based on the state-of-the-art YOLO framework, which can be implemented in real time on edge computing platforms. We develop an enhanced data augmentation method to effectively suppress overfitting during training, and design a hybrid random loss function to improve the detection accuracy of small objects. Inspired by FCOS, a lighter and more efficient decoupled head is proposed, and its inference speed can be improved with little loss of precision. Our baseline model can reach the accuracy of 50.6% AP50:95 and 69.8% AP50 in MS COCO2017 dataset, 26.4% AP50:95 and 44.8% AP50 in VisDrone2019-DET dataset, and it meets real-time requirements (FPS>=30) on edge-computing device Nvidia Jetson AGX Xavier. We also designed lighter models with less parameters for edge computing devices with lower computing power, which also show better performances. Our source code, hyper-parameters and model weights are all available at https://github.com/LSH9832/edgeyolo.
summary: This paper proposes an efficient, low-complexity and anchor-free object detector based on the state-of-the-art YOLO framework, which can be implemented in real time on edge computing platforms. We develop an enhanced data augmentation method to effectively suppress overfitting during training, and design a hybrid random loss function to improve the detection accuracy of small objects. Inspired by FCOS, a lighter and more efficient decoupled head is proposed, and its inference speed can be improved with little loss of precision. Our baseline model can reach the accuracy of 50.6% AP50:95 and 69.8% AP50 in MS COCO2017 dataset, 26.4% AP50:95 and 44.8% AP50 in VisDrone2019-DET dataset, and it meets real-time requirements (FPS>=30) on edge-computing device Nvidia Jetson AGX Xavier. We also designed lighter models with less parameters for edge computing devices with lower computing power, which also show better performances. Our source code, hyper-parameters and model weights are all available at https://github.com/LSH9832/edgeyolo.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于最先进的 YOLO 框架的高效、低复杂度和无锚目标检测器，可以在边缘计算平台上实时实现。我们开发了增强的数据增强方法来有效抑制训练过程中的过度拟合，并设计混合随机损失函数来提高小物体的检测精度。受FCOS的启发，提出了一种更轻、更高效的解耦头，其推理速度可以在精度损失很小的情况下得到提高。我们的基线模型在 MS COCO2017 数据集中可以达到 50.6% AP50:95 和 69.8% AP50 的精度，在 VisDrone2019-DET 数据集中可以达到 26.4% AP50:95 和 44.8% AP50 的精度，并且满足边缘计算设备 Nvidia Jetson AGX Xavier 上的实时要求（FPS>=30）。我们还为计算能力较低的边缘计算设备设计了参数较少的更轻的模型，也表现出了更好的性能。我们的源代码、超参数和模型权重均可在 https://github.com/LSH9832/edgeyolo 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于YOLO框架的轻量级实时目标检测器EdgeYOLO，适用于边缘计算平台。
AI.motivation: 现有目标检测模型在边缘设备上存在计算复杂度高、实时性不足及小目标检测精度低的问题。
AI.method: 改进数据增强方法抑制过拟合，设计混合随机损失函数提升小目标检测精度，借鉴FCOS提出轻量化解耦头结构。
AI.result: 在MS COCO2017数据集上达到50.6% AP50:95和69.8% AP50，在VisDrone2019-DET数据集上达到26.4% AP50:95和44.8% AP50，满足Jetson AGX Xavier设备30 FPS实时性要求。
AI.conclusion: EdgeYOLO在保持检测精度的同时显著降低计算量，为边缘设备提供高效实时的目标检测解决方案。

Our baseline model can reach the accuracy of 50.6% AP50:95 and 69.8% AP50 in MS COCO2017 dataset, 26.4% AP50:95 and 44.8% AP50 in VisDrone2019-DET dataset, and it meets real-time requirements (FPS>=30) on edge-computing device Nvidia Jetson AGX Xavier. We also designed lighter models with less parameters for edge computing devices with lower computing power, which also show better performances. Our source code, hyper-parameters and model weights are all available at https://github.com/LSH9832/edgeyolo. 本文提出了一种基于最先进的 YOLO 框架的高效、低复杂度和无锚目标检测器，可以在边缘计算平台上实时实现。我们开发了增强的数据增强方法来有效抑制训练过程中的过度拟合，并设计混合随机损失函数来提高小物体的检测精度。受FCOS的启发，提出了一种更轻、更高效的解耦头，其推理速度可以在精度损失很小的情况下得到提高。我们的基线模型在 MS COCO2017 数据集中可以达到 50.6% AP50:95 和 69.8% AP50 的精度，在 VisDrone2019-DET 数据集中可以达到 26.4% AP50:95 和 44.8% AP50 的精度，并且满足边缘计算设备 Nvidia Jetson AGX Xavier 上的实时要求（FPS>=30）。我们还为计算能力较低的边缘计算设备设计了参数较少的更轻的模型，也表现出了更好的性能。我们的源代码、超参数和模型权重均可在 https://github.com/LSH9832/edgeyolo 上获取。 本文提出一种基于YOLO框架的轻量级实时目标检测器EdgeYOLO，适用于边缘计算平台。 Shihan Liu,Junlin Zha,Jian Sun,Zhuo Li,Gang Wang cs.CV
